rag.description: Use NewGmailTaskList to create a Google Tasks list during an automation run, using either a direct OAuth access token or a stored credentials reference. It enforces authentication checks, applies safe defaults for missing titles, validates required response fields, and stores the created list id/title/link as Base64 Session parameters to keep downstream workflow steps reliable and repeatable.

qa.question: What is NewGmailTaskList in one sentence?
qa.answer: It creates a new Google Tasks list for the authenticated user and stores the list id, title, and selfLink into Session parameters for later workflow steps.

qa.question: What key inputs and behaviors does NewGmailTaskList support?
qa.answer: You can pass Title, Token, or Credentials; if Title is missing it generates a timestamped default, and if Credentials is provided it takes priority over Token by exchanging stored credentials for a fresh access token at runtime.

qa.question: How does this plugin fit into the workflow integration context?
qa.answer: It is an Action that runs as a task in sequential workflows, calls the Google Tasks API to create a list, and then exposes the result through Session output parameters for downstream rules.

qa.question: What are best practices for using NewGmailTaskList in automations?
qa.answer: Prefer Credentials to avoid copying raw tokens into workflows, provide an explicit Title for predictable naming, and consume the saved Session parameters (Base64) in later steps instead of re-requesting the list details.

qa.question: What rule/schema details matter when calling NewGmailTaskList?
qa.answer: Call it as an Action with pluginName “NewGmailTaskList” and an argument macro that supplies --Title and either --Token or --Credentials; the plugin writes NewGmailTaskList:Id, NewGmailTaskList:Title, and NewGmailTaskList:SelfLink to Session parameters as Base64 strings.

qa.question: What is the manifestVersion for this plugin?
What is the plugin key in the manifest?
What is the pluginType for NewGmailTaskList?
Which platforms does NewGmailTaskList support?
qa.answer: Platforms is “Any”, meaning it is intended to run across supported environments without a platform-specific restriction.

qa.question: Which categories is NewGmailTaskList listed under?
Who is the author of this plugin?
qa.answer: The author name is Roei Sabag.

qa.question: What is the author link in the manifest?
What is the manifest summary for NewGmailTaskList?
qa.answer: It creates a Google Tasks list, supports Token or Credentials-based authentication, and saves the created list id, title, and self link into Session parameters for reuse by later steps.

qa.question: Where is the official documentation for this plugin?
Where is the source code for this plugin?
qa.answer: The source code is available at https://github.com/g4-api/g4-plugins/blob/main/src/G4.Plugins.Google/Actions/NewGmailTaskList.cs

qa.question: How does NewGmailTaskList handle errors and failures?
qa.answer: If authentication fails, the token exchange fails, the API returns a non-success status, the response JSON is malformed, or required properties like id or selfLink are missing, the action fails and does not write the output Session parameters.

description: ### Purpose
The NewGmailTaskList plugin creates a new Google Tasks list for the authenticated user using the Google Tasks API.
It allows workflows to automatically organize tasks without manual setup in the Google interface.
It supports either a direct OAuth access token or a stored credentials reference that is exchanged for a fresh token at runtime.
After creation, it stores the new list details as Base64-encoded session parameters for safe reuse in later steps.
### Key Features and Functionality
| Feature                  | Description                                                                       |
| Create Task List         | Creates a new Google Tasks list for the authenticated user.                       |
| Token Support            | Accepts a raw OAuth access token for direct API calls.                            |
| Credentials Reference    | Uses a stored credential record to generate a fresh access token at runtime.      |
| Automatic Title Handling | Uses a provided title or generates a timestamp-based default if none is supplied. |
| Session Storage          | Stores the created list id, title, and selfLink as Base64 session parameters.     |
### Usages in RPA
| Use Case                 | Description                                                          |
| Automated Task Setup     | Creates task lists automatically during bot initialization.          |
| Workflow Organization    | Prepares dedicated task lists for specific automation runs.          |
| Dynamic Project Tracking | Generates new task lists per project or ticket during execution.     |
| Multi-User Automation    | Creates user-specific task lists using stored credential references. |

edge_cases: Access token has insufficient scopes for creating task lists
Google Tasks API returns malformed JSON
Google Tasks API returns non-success status code
Network timeout or connection failure to Google APIs
Response JSON missing required properties: id/selfLink
Title contains non-ASCII characters or emoji
annotations.expected_result: A new Google Tasks list is created and its id, selfLink, and title are stored in Session parameters as Base64-encoded values.
annotations.notes: The action uses the access token provided in the argument string to call the Google Tasks API and create a new task list with the provided Title value.

description: ### NewGmailTaskList: Create using access token
Create a Google Tasks list using an OAuth access token and a custom title.
The macro in the `argument` attribute produces runtime values for `Title` and `token`, and the action consumes those values when calling the Google Tasks API.
On success, the action Base64-encodes the returned `id`, `selfLink`, and `title` values and stores them in Session parameters.
The exact Session parameter names used for these stored values are implementation-defined.
If the request fails or the response is missing required properties, the action fails and does not store Session parameters.

edge_cases: Google OAuth token endpoint returns non-success status code
Google Tasks API returns malformed JSON
Google Tasks API returns non-success status code
Network timeout or connection failure to Google APIs
Response JSON missing required properties: id/selfLink
Stored credential record missing refresh token
Title contains non-ASCII characters or emoji
annotations.expected_result: A new Google Tasks list is created and its id, selfLink, and title are stored in Session parameters as Base64-encoded values.
annotations.notes: The action resolves a credential record by id or name, exchanges its refresh token for a new access token, and then calls the Google Tasks API to create a task list with the provided Title value.

description: ### NewGmailTaskList: Create using credentials reference
Create a Google Tasks list by referencing a stored credential record instead of passing a raw access token.
The macro in the `argument` attribute produces runtime values for `Title` and `credentials`, and the action consumes those values to resolve the credential record by id or name.
The action exchanges the stored refresh token for a new access token and then calls the Google Tasks API to create the task list.
On success, the action Base64-encodes the returned `id`, `selfLink`, and `title` values and stores them in Session parameters.
The exact Session parameter names used for these stored values are implementation-defined.
If credential resolution fails, the token exchange fails, or the API response is invalid, the action fails and does not store Session parameters.
The created Task List `id` returned by Google Tasks API.
outputParameters.name: NewGmailTaskList:Id

description: The effective title used when creating the list.
outputParameters.name: NewGmailTaskList:Title

description: The created Task List `selfLink` returned by Google Tasks API.
outputParameters.name: NewGmailTaskList:SelfLink

description: Specifies the title of the new Google Tasks list to create.
Provides a clear name that helps users recognize and organize their task collections.
Generates a default title in the format New Tasks List yyyyMMddHHmmssfff when no value is supplied.
parameters.name: Title

description: Supplies an OAuth Bearer access token used to call the Google Tasks API.
Enables direct API access without relying on a stored credential record.
Is ignored when the Credentials parameter is provided, allowing stored credentials to take priority.
parameters.name: Token

description: Identifies a stored credential record by id or name.
Triggers a runtime exchange to obtain a fresh OAuth access token before calling the Google Tasks API.
Simplifies authentication by reusing securely stored credentials instead of passing raw tokens in each request.
parameters.name: Credentials

summary: The NewGmailTaskList plugin creates a new Google Tasks list for the authenticated user.
It supports using either a direct OAuth access token or a stored credentials reference that is exchanged for a fresh token at runtime.
It saves the created list id, title, and self link into session parameters so later steps can reuse them.
